title: Phymosia rivularis
intro: Phymosia rivularis, commonly known as the Mexican Firecracker or Firecracker Bush, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phymosia rivularis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve cold and flu symptoms, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Phymosia rivularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Mexico and is also known as the 'Red Button Bush'. The plant is classified as a member of the subfamily Byttnerioideae. This classification places it within the larger clade of the Malvales order. Further, it is categorized as a part of the order Malvales and class Magnoliopsida.
